Evaluate the potential of India's startup ecosystem to drive economic growth and technological advancement in the coming decade.
GS3.imageDescription: Graphic showing a growing plant with coins, symbolizing seed funding for startups.
GS3.trivia: India is home to the third-largest startup ecosystem in the world, after the US and China.

GS3.headline: Government Proposes New Policy to Boost Medical Tourism and Healthcare Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Health and Family Welfare, in collaboration with the Ministry of Tourism, unveiled a draft policy aimed at positioning India as a global hub for medical and wellness tourism.
• The policy focuses on developing specialized medical infrastructure, promoting traditional Indian systems of medicine (AYUSH), and streamlining visa processes for medical tourists.
• Incentives for private hospitals and healthcare providers to upgrade facilities and achieve international accreditation are also part of the proposal.
• Marketing campaigns targeting specific regions and collaboration with foreign insurance providers are envisioned to attract a larger patient base.
• This initiative is expected to generate foreign exchange, create employment, and enhance India's soft power globally.

Mobilization of resources
Growth
Development and Employment
Health, Education, Human Resources.

Medical Value Travel (MVT) is a growing segment of tourism.
mainsPoints: Discuss the potential of medical and wellness tourism to contribute to India's economic growth, foreign exchange earnings, and employment generation.

GS3.trivia: India offers medical treatment at a fraction of the cost compared to Western countries, making it attractive for medical tourists.

GS3.headline: India Strengthens Cybersecurity Framework for Critical Infrastructure
GS3.explainer:
• The National Critical Information Infrastructure Protection Centre (NCIIPC) announced new directives to enhance the cybersecurity posture of critical infrastructure sectors.
• The directives mandate stricter security audits, implementation of advanced threat detection systems, and regular training for personnel in sectors like energy, finance, and telecommunications.
• This move comes in response to increasing sophisticated cyberattacks targeting essential services globally and domestically.
• Emphasis is placed on real-time threat intelligence sharing and building resilience against state-sponsored and criminal cyber threats.
• The government aims to create a robust and secure digital ecosystem to protect national assets and citizen data.

Role of media and social networking sites in internal security challenges
Basics of cyber security
Money-laundering and its prevention.

CERT-In (Indian Computer Emergency Response Team) is the nodal agency for responding to cyber security incidents.
Critical infrastructure sectors include power, banking, telecom, transport, and government.
mainsPoints: Discuss the growing threat of cyberattacks to India's critical infrastructure and its implications for national security and economic stability.
Analyze the key measures outlined in the new directives by NCIIPC to enhance cybersecurity resilience in vital sectors.
Examine the challenges in implementing a robust cybersecurity framework across diverse critical infrastructure organizations in India.
Suggest a multi-stakeholder approach involving government, private sector, and international cooperation to combat cyber threats effectively.

GS3.trivia: The Stuxnet computer worm, discovered in 2010, was one of the first known cyberweapons designed to target industrial control systems.

GS3.headline: Government Unveils New Policy to Boost India's Agricultural Exports
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Commerce and Industry introduced a new Agriculture Export Policy aimed at doubling India's agricultural exports by 2027.
• The policy focuses on diversifying the export basket, promoting value-added products, and exploring new markets for Indian agricultural produce.
• Key interventions include developing export-oriented infrastructure, promoting organic and GI-tagged products, and addressing sanitary and phytosanitary (SPS) barriers.
• It seeks to integrate farmers into the global value chain and ensure better price realization for their produce.
• The initiative is expected to enhance farmers' income, reduce post-harvest losses, and strengthen India's position in global agricultural trade.

E-technology in the aid of farmers.

India is a major producer of several agricultural commodities like spices, rice, and fruits.
The policy aims to address issues like lack of market access, infrastructure, and quality standards.
mainsPoints: Analyze the challenges and opportunities for India in enhancing its agricultural exports and achieving the target of doubling them by 2027.
Discuss the key strategies outlined in the new Agriculture Export Policy, including value addition, market diversification, and infrastructure development.
Examine how this policy can contribute to increasing farmers' income and reducing post-harvest losses in India.
Evaluate the role of quality standards, certifications, and addressing SPS barriers in making Indian agricultural products competitive in global markets.

GS3.trivia: India is the world's largest producer of spices, pulses, and milk.

GS3.headline: Scientists Discover Multiple New Species in Western Ghats Biodiversity Hotspot
GS3.explainer:
• Researchers from the Zoological Survey of India (ZSI) and various universities announced the discovery of several new species of flora and fauna in the Western Ghats.
• The discoveries include new species of amphibians, insects, and flowering plants, underscoring the immense biodiversity of the region.
• The Western Ghats is recognized as one of the world's eight 'hottest' biodiversity hotspots, facing significant threats from habitat loss and climate change.
• These findings emphasize the urgent need for enhanced conservation efforts and protection of critical habitats.
• The new species highlight the unexplored biological wealth and the importance of continued scientific research in these ecologically sensitive areas.
gsSubTopics: Conservation, environmental pollution and degradation, environmental impact assessment.
Biodiversity
prelimsPoints: The Western Ghats is a UNESCO World Heritage Site and one of the 36 global biodiversity hotspots.
It stretches along the western coast of India, traversing six states.
ZSI (Zoological Survey of India) is India's premier organization for zoological research.
Many species found here are endemic, meaning they are found nowhere else in the world.
mainsPoints: Discuss the ecological significance of the Western Ghats as a biodiversity hotspot and the threats it faces from anthropogenic activities and climate change.
Analyze the importance of discovering new species for conservation biology and understanding ecosystem health.
Examine the various conservation initiatives undertaken by the government and civil society to protect the Western Ghats' unique biodiversity.
Suggest integrated approaches for sustainable development and biodiversity conservation in ecologically sensitive regions like the Western Ghats.
GS3.imageDescription: Lush green landscape of the Western Ghats.
GS3.trivia: The Western Ghats are older than the Himalayan mountain range.

GS3.headline: India's Solar Energy Capacity Crosses 100 GW Milestone with New Installations
GS3.explainer:
• India has officially surpassed 100 GW of installed solar power capacity, marking a significant achievement in its renewable energy transition.
• This milestone was driven by robust policy support, increasing private investment, and declining solar panel costs.
• The new installations include large-scale solar parks, rooftop solar projects, and off-grid solutions, contributing to energy access in remote areas.
• The government reiterates its commitment to achieving 500 GW of non-fossil fuel energy capacity by 2030.
• This expansion strengthens India's energy security, reduces carbon emissions, and positions the country as a leader in solar energy deployment.

Infrastructure: Energy

The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was co-founded by India.
Rajasthan and Gujarat are leading states in solar power generation.
National Solar Mission (NSM) is a major government initiative for solar energy.
mainsPoints: Discuss the significance of India crossing the 100 GW solar capacity milestone for its energy security and climate change commitments.
Analyze the key drivers and policy frameworks that have facilitated the rapid growth of solar energy in India.
Examine the challenges that remain in scaling up renewable energy adoption, including grid integration, storage, and financing.

GS3.imageDescription: Vast solar panel farm under a sunny sky.
GS3.trivia: Bhadla Solar Park in Rajasthan is one of the largest solar power plants in the world.

GS3.headline: Government Finalizes National AI Policy Framework, Focus on Ethical Development
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Electronics and Information Technology (MeitY) announced the finalization of India's National Artificial Intelligence (AI) Policy Framework.
• The policy emphasizes 'AI for All' with a focus on inclusive growth, ethical AI development, and responsible deployment across sectors.
• Key pillars include fostering R&D, building a skilled AI workforce, ensuring data governance, and promoting public-private partnerships.
• The framework addresses concerns regarding job displacement, algorithmic bias, and privacy, proposing regulatory sandboxes and ethical guidelines.
• India aims to leverage AI for economic transformation, social empowerment, and solving grand challenges in health, agriculture, and education.

AI is being deployed in sectors like healthcare, agriculture, education, and smart cities.

Analyze the ethical considerations and challenges associated with AI development and deployment, such as algorithmic bias, privacy, and job displacement.

Evaluate the role of public-private partnerships and international collaboration in fostering a responsible and inclusive AI ecosystem in India.

GS3.trivia: The term 'Artificial Intelligence' was coined by John McCarthy in 1956.

GS3.headline: Government Launches New Schemes to Promote Millet Cultivation and Consumption
GS3.explainer:
• The Ministry of Agriculture and Farmers Welfare introduced a series of new initiatives to boost the cultivation, processing, and consumption of millets across India.
• The schemes include financial incentives for farmers, promotion of millet-based food products, and extensive awareness campaigns about their nutritional benefits.
• This aligns with India's declaration of 2023 as the 'International Year of Millets' and its ongoing efforts to revive traditional grains.
• Focus is also on improving market linkages, developing value chains, and supporting FPOs (Farmer Producer Organizations) involved in millet production.
• The move aims to enhance food security, address malnutrition, and promote sustainable agriculture practices.

Millets are also known as 'nutri-cereals' and are drought-resistant.
